Test case description: Enable browser push in notification settings
Expected behavior: Service worker registered. Subscription POSTed to /api/push-subscriptions. Button changes to Disable.

Test case description: Disable browser push in notification settings
Expected behavior: DELETE /api/push-subscriptions called. Subscription removed. Button changes to Enable.

Test case description: Multiple devices per user independent subscriptions
Steps: 1. Subscribe on browser A and browser B (same user).
Expected behavior: Two rows in web_push_subscriptions unique by endpoint. Both receive pushes.

Steps: 1. DB has subscription with expired/410 endpoint. 2. Trigger notification.
Expected behavior: sendWebPush catches 404/410, deletes stale rows from DB. Other subscriptions still receive.

Test case description: Push no-ops when VAPID keys not configured
Steps: 1. Unset VAPID env vars in dev. 2. Trigger an event.
Expected behavior: sendWebPush returns immediately without error. No crash.

Test case description: RLS: users can only manage own push subscriptions
Steps: 1. User A subscribes. 2. User B tries to GET/DELETE user A's subscription.
Expected behavior: RLS policy rejects. Only own rows accessible.

Test case description: Push notification delivered on request approval
Steps: 1. User subscribed to push. Admin approves their request.
Expected behavior: Push notification received in browser with correct title and link.

Test case description: Browser without push API gracefully hidden
Steps: 1. Open notification settings in a browser without PushManager support.
Expected behavior: Enable/Disable push UI section hidden. No JS error.
Page renders grouped by module (Haraka, Raseed, Requests, etc.). In-app + email toggles per event.

Test case description: Catalog defaults shown when no user preference row
Steps: 1. Fresh user with no notification_preferences rows. 2. Open preferences.
Expected behavior: Each event shows catalog's defaultInApp / defaultEmail as effective value.

Test case description: Org notification defaults override catalog defaults
Steps: 1. Insert notification_org_defaults row for an event with custom values. 2. User with no pref opens preferences.
Expected behavior: Effective value follows org default, not catalog default.

Test case description: User preference overrides org and catalog defaults
Steps: 1. Save a user preference (in-app=false for an event). 2. Reload page.
Expected behavior: That event shows in-app=false. Other events still follow org/catalog defaults.
PATCH persists rows to notification_preferences. Toast 'Notification preferences saved'.

Test case description: In-app notification inserted when event triggered
Steps: 1. User with in-app enabled for event X. 2. Trigger event X.
Expected behavior: Row inserted in notifications table for that recipient with event_type, title, link.

Test case description: Email sent when recipient opts in for email
Steps: 1. User with email=true for event X. 2. Trigger event X.
Expected behavior: Email dispatched via Resend with subject, HTML body including link.

Test case description: Notification not sent to user with in-app=false
Steps: 1. User sets in-app=false for event X. 2. Trigger event X.
Expected behavior: No notification row inserted for that user.

Test case description: Recipient resolution by org role defaults
Expected behavior: Only admin-role org members receive in-app notification.

Test case description: Notification queue fire-and-forget: errors do not throw
Steps: 1. Simulate DB failure during notification insert. 2. Trigger event from service.
Expected behavior: Error logged internally. Calling service continues; no exception propagated.

Test case description: Notification bell + in-app inbox visible in header
Steps: 1. Trigger event that inserts notification. 2. Check app header.
Expected behavior: Unread count badge shown on bell. Clicking shows notification in inbox.
is_read=true. Unread count decreases.

Test case description: Paymob card terminal: full 4-step charge flow
Steps: 1. Configure Paymob integration (api_key, integration_id). 2. Initiate card charge in POS.
Expected behavior: Provider executes: auth → order → payment_key → terminal pay. Returns approved/declined/pending.
Throws 'Paymob auth failed'. POS shows error toast.

Steps: 1. Auth succeeds; order endpoint returns 500. 2. Initiate charge.
Expected behavior: Throws with status code. Error surfaced to user.

Steps: 1. Auth + order succeed; payment_key endpoint fails.
Expected behavior: Throws with status code. No orphan order left in POS state.
ProviderChargeResponse approved=true, status='approved'.
approved=false, status='declined'. POS shows decline message.

Test case description: Paymob charge returns pending when ambiguous
Steps: 1. Terminal charge responds without success field.
Expected behavior: status='pending'. POS allows manual status check.

Steps: 1. checkStatus with valid providerRef. 2. Paymob returns success=true.
Expected behavior: Returns approved=true, providerRef updated.
verifyWebhook returns {reference, providerRef, status:'approved'}.

Test case description: Paymob webhook with invalid/empty body returns null
Steps: 1. POST webhook with malformed JSON or missing obj.reference.
Expected behavior: verifyWebhook returns null. No crash.
